maxtoki-mlx torch_pipeline
  • scripts.torch_pipeline.spec
  • View page source

scripts.torch_pipeline.spec

Spec for what to feed the model in a zero-shot perturbation experiment.

The spec answers four questions per row of the prepared dataset:

  1. Which cell is the QUERY (the cell whose temporal prediction will shift under perturbation)?

  2. Which cells form the CONTEXT (the trajectory of past cells the model conditions on, in pseudotime order)?

  3. WHICH GENE is being KO’d / inhibited / overexpressed?

  4. Where is the perturbation applied – query only, or also in context?

A spec is normally written as YAML / JSON and loaded with load_spec. Programmatic construction works too – it’s just a dataclass.

YAML example:

data:
  h5ad: ./data/4111_cells.h5ad
  pseudotime_col: pseudotime         # obs column used for ordering
  group_col: donor_id                # cells of the same group share a trajectory
  cell_id_col: null                  # defaults to obs_names
  count_col: n_counts                # null -> compute from X.sum()

context:
  strategy: prefix                   # self | prefix | all_in_group | explicit
  max_cells: 4                       # cap context length (in cells, not tokens)
  ordering: pseudotime               # pseudotime | obs_index
  include_self: false                # when prefix/all_in_group, include the query?
  explicit_indices: null             # list[int] when strategy == explicit

query:
  strategy: each_cell                # each_cell | latest_per_group
  filter_obs:                        # optional - keep only cells matching these
    cell_type: ["fibroblast"]

perturbation:
  gene_symbol: ZLX1                  # one of gene / gene_symbol must be set
  gene: null                         # Ensembl ID; takes precedence if set
  direction: inhibit                 # inhibit | delete | overexpress
  apply_to: query                    # query | query_and_context

Set context.strategy: self for the simple cell-vs-itself baseline (equivalent to the previous default). Set query.strategy: latest_per_group to score one prediction per donor / sample instead of per cell.

class scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.DataSpec(h5ad: str, pseudotime_col: str | None = None, group_col: str | None = None, cell_id_col: str | None = None, count_col: str | None = 'n_counts')[source]

Bases: object

Where the cells live and which obs columns drive selection.

h5ad

Path to a single .h5ad file. Counts must live in adata.X or adata.raw.X; gene IDs in var["ensembl_id"], var["feature_id"], or ENSG-prefixed var_names.

Type:

str

pseudotime_col

Name of an obs column with monotone pseudotime / age / observation-time values. Required by context strategies prefix and all_in_group, and by pool_select.sort_by="pseudotime".

Type:

str | None

group_col

Name of an obs column that groups cells into trajectories (e.g. donor_id). Required by prefix and all_in_group.

Type:

str | None

cell_id_col

Name of an obs column to use as the per-cell ID in output records. None falls back to adata.obs_names.

Type:

str | None

count_col

Name of an obs column holding pre-computed total UMI counts per cell. None re-computes from the count matrix.

Type:

str | None

h5ad: str
pseudotime_col: str | None = None
group_col: str | None = None
cell_id_col: str | None = None
count_col: str | None = 'n_counts'
class scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.PoolSelect(n: int = 3, pick: Literal['first', 'last', 'evenly_spaced', 'random'] = 'evenly_spaced', sort_by: Literal['pseudotime', 'obs_index'] = 'pseudotime', seed: int = 0)[source]

Bases: object

How to choose context cells from a filtered pool (one pool, reused for every query).

n: int = 3
pick: Literal['first', 'last', 'evenly_spaced', 'random'] = 'evenly_spaced'
sort_by: Literal['pseudotime', 'obs_index'] = 'pseudotime'
seed: int = 0
class scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.ContextSpec(strategy: ~typing.Literal['self', 'prefix', 'all_in_group', 'explicit', 'pool'] = 'self', max_cells: int = 4, ordering: ~typing.Literal['pseudotime', 'obs_index'] = 'pseudotime', include_self: bool = False, explicit_indices: list[int] | None = None, pool_filter: dict[str, list[~typing.Any]] = <factory>, pool_select: ~scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.PoolSelect | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

How the trajectory of past cells (the context) is assembled per query.

strategy

One of self (cell vs. itself), prefix (same-group cells with smaller pseudotime), all_in_group (all other cells in same group), explicit (fixed adata indices for every query), or pool (filter then pick from a cross-group pool).

Type:

Literal[‘self’, ‘prefix’, ‘all_in_group’, ‘explicit’, ‘pool’]

max_cells

Final cap on number of context cells per row.

Type:

int

ordering

How context cells are ordered in the input sequence.

Type:

Literal[‘pseudotime’, ‘obs_index’]

include_self

When True and strategy is prefix / all_in_group, append the query itself to the context.

Type:

bool

explicit_indices

adata.obs row indices used as context for every query. Required when strategy == "explicit".

Type:

list[int] | None

pool_filter

{obs_col: [allowed_values]} filter applied to the full cell list. Required when strategy == "pool".

Type:

dict[str, list[Any]]

pool_select

How to pick a fixed-size context from the filtered pool. Required when strategy == "pool".

Type:

scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.PoolSelect | None

strategy: Literal['self', 'prefix', 'all_in_group', 'explicit', 'pool'] = 'self'
max_cells: int = 4
ordering: Literal['pseudotime', 'obs_index'] = 'pseudotime'
include_self: bool = False
explicit_indices: list[int] | None = None
pool_filter: dict[str, list[Any]]
pool_select: PoolSelect | None = None
class scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.QuerySpec(strategy: ~typing.Literal['each_cell', 'latest_per_group'] = 'each_cell', filter_obs: dict[str, list[~typing.Any]] = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Which cells become queries (one row each in the output dataset).

strategy

each_cell -> one row per cell; latest_per_group -> one row per group, the cell with the largest pseudotime.

Type:

Literal[‘each_cell’, ‘latest_per_group’]

filter_obs

{obs_col: [allowed_values]} to score only matching cells (e.g. {"cell_type": ["fibroblast"]}).

Type:

dict[str, list[Any]]

strategy: Literal['each_cell', 'latest_per_group'] = 'each_cell'
filter_obs: dict[str, list[Any]]
class scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.PerturbationSpec(gene: str | None = None, gene_symbol: str | None = None, direction: Literal['inhibit', 'delete', 'overexpress'] = 'inhibit', apply_to: Literal['query', 'query_and_context'] = 'query')[source]

Bases: object

Which gene to perturb, how, and where it’s applied in the row.

gene

Ensembl ID (e.g. "ENSG00000109906"). Takes precedence over gene_symbol if both are set.

Type:

str | None

gene_symbol

HGNC symbol (e.g. "ZLX1"). Resolved against the packaged gene_name_id.json.

Type:

str | None

direction

inhibit moves the gene’s token to the lowest rank; delete removes it; overexpress moves it to the front.

Type:

Literal[‘inhibit’, ‘delete’, ‘overexpress’]

apply_to

query perturbs only the query cell; query_and_context also perturbs every cell in the context.

Type:

Literal[‘query’, ‘query_and_context’]

gene: str | None = None
gene_symbol: str | None = None
direction: Literal['inhibit', 'delete', 'overexpress'] = 'inhibit'
apply_to: Literal['query', 'query_and_context'] = 'query'
class scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.ExperimentSpec(data: ~scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.DataSpec, context: ~scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.ContextSpec = <factory>, query: ~scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.QuerySpec = <factory>, perturbation: ~scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.PerturbationSpec = <factory>, seq_length: int = 16384)[source]

Bases: object

Top-level spec for one zero-shot perturbation experiment.

A spec is normally written as YAML (see configs/ for examples) and loaded via load_spec(). It can also be constructed programmatically.

data

Input h5ad + obs-column mapping.

Type:

scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.DataSpec

context

How the trajectory of past cells is assembled per query.

Type:

scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.ContextSpec

query

Which cells are scored.

Type:

scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.QuerySpec

perturbation

Gene KO / inhibition / overexpression target.

Type:

scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.PerturbationSpec

seq_length

Model context length. Default 16384 for trajectory tasks; the per-cell rank-value cap is computed from this so the worst-case row fits.

Type:

int

data: DataSpec
context: ContextSpec
query: QuerySpec
perturbation: PerturbationSpec
seq_length: int = 16384
validate() → None[source]

Raise ValueError if the spec is internally inconsistent.

to_dict() → dict[source]
scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.load_spec(path: str | Path) → ExperimentSpec[source]

Load an ExperimentSpec from a YAML or JSON file.

Parameters:

path – Filesystem path. Suffix decides the parser (.yaml / .yml -> PyYAML, anything else -> json).

Returns:

A validated ExperimentSpec.

Raises:

ValueError – If the spec fails validation.

scripts.torch_pipeline.spec.spec_from_dict(raw: dict) → ExperimentSpec[source]

Build an ExperimentSpec from a nested dict (typically parsed YAML).
